title: thymus chamaedrys var. comosus
intro: Thymus chamaedrys var. comosus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe, with tiny, fragrant leaves and small, pinkish-purple flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems and promote wound healing. Creeping thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in potpourri, perfumes, and culinary dishes.
